Container.Name: traefik-manager
Container.Overview: A web UI for managing Traefik routes, middlewares, services, and certificates. Supports multiple config files, API keys for mobile access, two-factor authentication, and config backups. Requires access to your Traefik dynamic config file and the Traefik API.
Container.Requires: Traefik must be running and its API must be reachable from this container.

Config.Name: Web UI Port
Config.Description: Port to access the Traefik Manager web UI.

Config.Name: Config Directory
Config.Description: Stores manager.yml (settings, password, API keys) and the session signing key. Must be persistent.

Config.Name: Backups Directory
Config.Description: Timestamped backups of your dynamic config are saved here before every change.

Config.Name: Traefik API URL
Config.Description: URL of the Traefik API. Usually http://traefik:8080 when on the same Docker network.

Config.Name: Domains
Config.Description: Comma-separated list of base domains shown in the Add Route form. Example: example.com,home.lab

Config.Name: Cert Resolver
Config.Description: Default ACME certificate resolver name from your traefik.yml. Comma-separate multiple resolvers.

Config.Name: Config File Path (single file)
Config.Description: Path inside the container to your Traefik dynamic config file. Use this for a single config file. Mount the file via the path mapping below. Leave blank if using CONFIG_DIR or CONFIG_PATHS.

Config.Name: Config Directory Path (multi-file)
Config.Description: Path inside the container to a directory containing multiple .yml config files. All .yml files in this directory will be loaded. Use instead of CONFIG_PATH when managing multiple files. Leave blank if using CONFIG_PATH.

Config.Name: Config Paths (explicit list)
Config.Description: Comma-separated list of full config file paths inside the container. Use instead of CONFIG_PATH for 2-5 named files. Example: /app/config/routes.yml,/app/config/services.yml. Leave blank if using CONFIG_PATH or CONFIG_DIR.

Config.Name: Admin Password
Config.Description: Optional: set a fixed admin password. If left empty a temporary password is generated on first start - check the container log for it.

Config.Name: Cookie Secure
Config.Description: Set to true when accessing Traefik Manager over HTTPS (e.g. behind a Traefik reverse proxy with TLS). Required for session cookies to work correctly over HTTPS.

Config.Name: Auth Enabled
Config.Description: Set to false to disable built-in login. Only use this when Traefik Manager is protected by an external auth provider such as Authentik or Authelia.

Config.Name: Settings File Path
Config.Description: Path inside the container to the Traefik Manager settings file. Change this if you want to store it separately from the dynamic config.

Config.Name: Backup Directory Path
Config.Description: Path inside the container where backups are written. Change this if you mount a custom backup volume at a different path.

Config.Name: Inactivity Timeout (minutes)
Config.Description: Minutes of inactivity before a non-remembered session is logged out.

Config.Name: OTP Encryption Key
Config.Description: Fernet key for encrypting the 2FA TOTP secret at rest. Auto-generated on first start if not set. Set this if you want to manage the key yourself or ensure it survives config volume replacement. Generate with: python3 -c "from cryptography.fernet import Fernet; print(Fernet.generate_key().decode())"

Config.Name: Secret Key
Config.Description: Session signing key. Auto-generated on first start and saved to /app/config/.secret_key. Set this explicitly to ensure sessions survive a full config wipe, or to share a key across multiple instances.
